behavior_summary: U8P_FIRESWMP::FIRESWMP::look: When the player (or relevant actor) examines the fire-swamp object, the script prepares context and displays a short description text "A strange hole." via Item::bark. This is purely a descriptive 'U8P_FIRESWMP::look' handler with no world-state changes.

U8P_FIRESWMP::FIRESWMP::gotHit: When the object is hit (Event 6), the handler enumerates a nearby collection of items/actors, filters candidates (calls Item::isNpc), and for the first matching candidate it computes an offset position (candidate X+0x10, Y+0x10, Z) and spawns a fire-spell effect (spawn FIRESPEL::1F65) at that location. It then invokes METHOD::0080 on the context/target (likely to apply an effect) and schedules a cleanup timer (spawn FREE::1C19 with delay 0x64). The routine loops through candidates and suspends/returns after applying the effect.
notable_control_flow: U8P_FIRESWMP::FIRESWMP::look: single-entry, immediate call to Item::bark to show a description, then cleanup/return.

U8P_FIRESWMP::FIRESWMP::gotHit: loop over a collection of nearby items/actors using iterator locals ([BP-09h] etc.), check Item::isNpc on each candidate ([BP-07h]), and on match compute coordinates and spawn a FIRESPEL effect followed by a METHOD::0080 call and a delayed FREE spawn. The handler returns/suspends after applying on a candidate.
Effect application path is guarded by the Item::isNpc check and candidate iteration; only matching candidates receive the spawn+METHOD flow.
open_questions: What exact collection is iterated by U8P_FIRESWMP::FIRESWMP::gotHit (nearby actors, inventory, or a global actor list)?
What are the precise semantics of METHOD::0080 (the two byte arguments 0x05 and 0x08 and how they alter the target)?
What do the numeric offsets (+0x10 for X and Y) represent in gameplay terms (visual offset, spawn grid offset, or alignment)?
Are the U8P_FIRESWMP::FIRESWMP::gotHit parameters (uword, word) the attacker id and damage/type, and are they used outside the shown excerpt?
Does the spawned FREE::1C19 free the spawned FIRESPEL entity or perform other cleanup tied to the pid/context?

main_function.name: U8P_FIRESWMP::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the textual description when the player examines the fire-swamp object; set up local item info/context and call Item::bark to present the string "A strange hole." to the viewer.
entry_conditions: Event 0 - invoked when an actor looks at / examines the object (player 'U8P_FIRESWMP::look' event)

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESWMP::look
function_map.role: Examine/U8P_FIRESWMP::look handler that displays a short description string via Item::bark.

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESWMP::gotHit
function_map.role: Hit/event handler (Event 6) that searches candidates, filters NPCs, spawns a fire-spell effect at an offset of a matched candidate's position, applies an effect via METHOD::0080, and schedules cleanup.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine routine used to display a short message/string from an item to a PID/process.

function_map.name: Item::isNpc
function_map.role: Query whether a candidate item is an NPC (used to filter valid targets).

function_map.name: Item::getX
function_map.role: Return an item's X coordinate for spawn-position computation.

function_map.name: Item::getY
function_map.role: Return an item's Y coordinate for spawn-position computation.

function_map.name: Item::getZ
function_map.role: Return an item's Z/level coordinate for spawn-position computation.

function_map.name: spawn FIRESPEL::1F65
function_map.role: Spawn a fire-spell/effect entity at computed coordinates (visual/attack effect).

function_map.name: METHOD::0080
function_map.role: Apply a method/engine operation (likely effect/application) with small-byte args to the target/context after spawning the effect.

function_map.name: spawn FREE::1C19
function_map.role: Schedule a delayed cleanup/free action (passed pid and a delay of 0x64).

external_calls.purpose: Deliver the descriptive string "A strange hole." to the viewing process/pid so the player sees the item's short description.
Filter iteration candidates to NPCs before applying the fire effect.
Read candidate X coordinate to compute spawn X = candidate X + 0x10.
Read candidate Y coordinate to compute spawn Y = candidate Y + 0x10.
Read candidate Z coordinate to use as spawn Z.
Create/play a fire-spell effect at the computed coordinates to represent the hit/attack consequence.
Invoke an effect/application API on the context and target, likely to apply damage/status/flag related to the spawned fire effect.
Schedule a delayed cleanup/free operation associated with the spawned effect/context (delay 0x64).

This is purely a descriptive 'U8P_FIRESWMP::look' handler with no world-state changes.
